user_objective: Initialize a persistent local-first, repo-backed memory system that preserves PolitiViral project state across sessions.
actions_taken: Inspected the PolitiViral repo root, package.json, README.md, Supabase function structure, git branch, and recent commits.
Confirmed there was no existing project-memory system in the repo.
Reconstructed the active product, design, auth, deployment, and ops state from the repo and recent confirmed work.
Created the canonical project-memory/ folder and seeded the eight core JSON memory files.
key_findings: PolitiViral is on a clean standalone repo and recent milestone 1 work is already pushed to main.
The live auth email system is partially configured: branded templates and hook are in place, but sender-domain delivery is blocked by the current Resend plan/domain slot.
The onboarding redirect loop that made setup/profile links feel dead has already been fixed in code.
memory_updates: Created the initial durable project profile, goals, current state, task backlog, file map, and knowledge registry.
Recorded live auth infrastructure status and the current Resend blocker so future sessions do not repeat discovery.
unresolved_items: Decide whether to temporarily switch auth email sending to byoroofer.com, disable the hook, or upgrade/fix Resend so politiviral.com can be verified.
Run the end-to-end auth email verification checklist after sender delivery is stable.
next_recommended_actions: Resolve the auth email sender-domain blocker first because it affects live sign-up and recovery reliability.
Propagate the homepage design system to /for-campaigns, /for-creators, and /pricing next.

user_objective: Scan the repo, diagnose the reported live application errors, and repair the issue without losing project context.
actions_taken: Loaded the canonical project-memory files before continuing work.
Ran project verification checks and confirmed npm run typecheck and npm run build both succeeded.
Checked live public routes and confirmed /, /signup, /login, and /reset-password all returned 200 OK.
Reviewed auth, onboarding, profile, and migration files to rule out repo-mix or route-level breakage.
Confirmed the live failure mode was auth email delivery from an unverified sender domain rather than a crashing app build.
Updated live Supabase function secrets to use the verified fallback sender PolitiViral <no-reply@byoroofer.com>.
Verified direct Resend sending succeeded with the fallback sender and verified a Supabase signup smoke test returned error: null.
key_findings: The repo itself is not broken: typecheck, production build, and primary public routes all passed.
The reported live problem was auth email delivery risk caused by the send-email hook using no-reply@politiviral.com before that domain was verified in Resend.
A temporary switch to the verified byoroofer.com sender restored branded auth delivery without code changes.
memory_updates: Updated current state to reflect that the live issue was diagnosed as auth-email infrastructure rather than code/runtime breakage.
Recorded the temporary byoroofer.com sender fallback, the verification results, and the remaining permanent-domain work.
unresolved_items: Verify the remaining live auth email flows beyond signup smoke testing: magic link, password reset, invite, email change, and reauthentication.
Decide whether to upgrade or reconfigure Resend so politiviral.com can become the permanent verified sender domain.
Rotate the exposed Supabase and Resend credentials after the rollout stabilizes.
next_recommended_actions: Run the full auth email checklist while the temporary byoroofer.com sender is active.
Choose the permanent sender-domain plan for no-reply@politiviral.com.
Continue the secondary marketing-page polish after auth delivery is fully stable.

user_objective: Diagnose and repair the live signup-page client-side exception on politiviral.com.
actions_taken: Loaded the canonical PolitiViral project-memory files before debugging the new incident.
Inspected the signup route, auth components, Supabase env helpers, and related layout/runtime files.
Pulled the Vercel production environment into a temporary local file and confirmed the public Supabase and app URL values contained embedded newline characters.
Patched public env handling to trim whitespace and accidental quote wrappers.
Moved Supabase browser client creation out of auth component render paths and into submit handlers for signup, login, magic link, password recovery, and password update.
Deleted the temporary pulled env file after inspection.
Verified npm run typecheck and npm run build both passed.
Deployed the patched build to Vercel production and confirmed the alias returned to https://politiviral.com.
key_findings: The production public env values for NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY contained embedded newline characters.
Auth pages were instantiating the Supabase browser client during render, which turned malformed env values into immediate client-side page crashes.
A combination of env sanitation and lazy browser-client initialization fixed the issue and was deployed live.
memory_updates: Recorded the Vercel env-value formatting issue, the deployed runtime hardening patch, and the new follow-up task to clean the dashboard values at the source.
unresolved_items: Clean the malformed Vercel public env values in the dashboard so future deploys do not depend on code-side trimming alone.
Verify the live signup, login, and reset-password pages after CDN propagation.
Continue the remaining auth email verification checklist and permanent sender-domain work.
next_recommended_actions: Clean the Vercel production env values for the public Supabase URL, public key, and app URL.
Open the live signup and login pages and confirm the browser exception is gone.
Then continue the broader auth verification checklist.
